title: Aptitude: Trigonometry & Height and Distances - Q3

content.problemStatement: ### **Aptitude Practice Question** There are two poles, one on each side of the road. The higher pole is 54 m high. From the top of this pole, the angles of depression of the top and bottom of the shorter pole are 30 and 60 degrees, respectively. Find the height of the shorter pole.Let AB and CD be the two poles. Let AC = x m and CD = h mNow, in triangle ABC, tan 60 = AB / AC √3 = 54 / AC AC = 18 √3 m Clearly, AC = DE = 18 √3 m In triangle BED, tan 30 = BE / DE BE = DE tan 30 BE = 18 √3 / √3 m BE = 18 m CD = AE = AB – BE CD = 54 – 18 = 36 m Therefore, the height of the shorter pole = 36 m
